rows.concept: One static Go binary (the whole governed surface)
rows.what_you_get: A single dependency-free Go binary (no Python, no CUDA, no go.sum) that you `go build` and run — the gateway, policy gate, quarantine, audit log, and metrics all in one process.
rows.witness: go build ./... exit 0; cross-platform CI (ubuntu/macos/windows); zero go.sum
gaps: Optional -tags vulkan/cuda backends add GPU dependencies (off by default)
Production serving (continuous batching, paged attention) still fronts an external engine
rows.durability_note: CI builds + tests across three OSes on every push; the zero-dependency claim is structural (no go.sum entries in the default build).

rows.what_you_get: Front any OpenAI-compatible model server with one command; every tool call the model proposes is denied / repaired / quarantined before your agent runs it, with /metrics and /healthz.
rows.witness: internal/gateway tests; TestServedResultArmsResultSideStack; v0.2.1 adversarial hardening
gaps: Needs an upstream model server (ollama / vLLM / a provider) running; the offline feel is the no-server boundary (fak agent --offline / serve --stdio)
stream:true SSE is synthesized from the finished adjudicated turn, not true token-by-token streaming
rows.durability_note: Gateway tests run on every commit; the wire client never supplies a Ref, so the IFC/secret/self-modify rungs stay armed on proxied calls.

rows.what_you_get: Expose the kernel's verdict as MCP tools (fak_adjudicate, fak_admit, ...) over stdio so Claude Code or any MCP client can ask the kernel before it runs a tool — offline, no upstream model needed.
rows.witness: internal/gateway tests; MCP spec-conformance + v0.2.1 hardening; examples/mcp/.mcp.json
gaps: OpenAI-wire MCP bridging is unit-tested but has no captured end-to-end transcript
rows.durability_note: The /mcp HTTP route and the stdio path exercise the same in-process kernel boundary tested on every build.

rows.what_you_get: One command wraps a real Claude Code session with the capability floor armed — your own key and prompt-cache breakpoints pass through untouched, and you get a verdict roll-up on exit.
gaps: Needs the claude CLI + an Anthropic key; the offline equivalent is `guard --dump-policy`
KV poison-eviction is a no-op on passthrough (the model lives upstream, no local KV to drop)
rows.durability_note: The exit audit reads the same /metrics counters, so it cannot overstate the protection; the embedded floor works from any directory.

rows.what_you_get: A runnable loop that evaluates a code candidate in an isolated worktree, measures a KPI, runs the suite, and KEEPS it only on a non-forgeable keep-bit (metric gain AND suite-green AND truth-clean).
gaps: The demo KPI is a deterministic LRU hit-rate; a real subsystem optimization plugs in via a custom Proposer/Measurer
Suite-green defaults to go build+vet (a Windows-safe proxy); the full test suite is WSL-only
rows.durability_note: The keep-bit is non-forgeable (witness + metrics); the loop never mutates main without an explicit gated land step; the journal is append-only.
